Cholesterol in LDL [Mass/volume] in Serum or Plasma by Direct assay
Cholesterol in HDL [Mass/volume] in Serum or Plasma

tr.th: Conclusion and Recommendations based on this report and previous findings known to us

tr.td: The patient shows mildly elevated HbA1c, suggesting impaired glucose tolerance or early diabetes. Lipid profile reveals high LDL and low HDL, indicating increased cardiovascular risk. Hemoglobin is slightly low, possibly mild anemia. Platelet distribution width is elevated, which may reflect platelet activation or variability. Renal function, electrolytes, and other parameters are within normal limits. Recommend clinical correlation and follow-up for glycemic control, anemia evaluation, and cardiovascular risk management.

display.value: Disorder of kidney due to diabetes mellitus
Microalbuminuria due to type 2 diabetes mellitus
